notes: Two real committed decks, processed the same way: the skill reads the rendered slide PNGs and transcribes each into a `## slide-NN` section (the deck PDFs are image-heavy exports, so reading the rendered slides is the universal path). kowa (21 slides) is the richer case — its real content includes text baked into editor/dashboard SCREENSHOTS (e.g. the `CLAUDE.md` editor view on slide 7, verified by eye) that a PDF text-layer extractor cannot read; only reading the rendered image recovers it. `CLAUDE.md` is therefore a trap term here: it is genuinely on the slide but ABSENT from the project's committed slides.txt, which was produced by the old text-layer parser (the committed file is byte-identical to that parser's output) — so the trap guards exactly the quality the png→text rewrite buys. khew (6 slides) is a smaller image-only deck. The discriminator vs a baseline told to ignore the skill is the OUTPUT CONTRACT (a slides.txt sibling, ## slide-NN one section per slide, exact-casing term preservation) — a capable baseline recovers the terms but not the convention. Graded deterministically by grade.py: section count == staged PNG count, structure/encoding invariants, per-term verbatim (case-sensitive) presence, and source files untouched. The committed slides.txt is NOT staged (prepare.py copies only slides.pdf + slides/). HARNESS CONTRACT: (1) run `python3 prepare.py runs/<iteration>` BEFORE spawning executors — prompts carry {WORK_DIR}, resolved into each run's eval_metadata.json and the printed run plan; (2) the skill IS installed in this workspace, so without_skill executors must be told to ignore the installed slides-png-to-text skill and extract the deck text however they would unaided (baseline-contamination guard); (3) after each executor finishes, write its token/duration notification to <run-dir>/timing.json — outputs are graded in place under <run-dir>/work/. with_skill executors run the skill, which writes work/slides.txt.

evals.name: kowa
evals.prompt: I have a talk's slide deck rendered as images in {WORK_DIR}/slides/ (the source PDF is at {WORK_DIR}/slides.pdf). Please pull the deck's text out into a single reusable plain-text file beside the PDF, so later steps can build a term glossary from it without re-reading the slide images. Keep the exact spelling and casing of product, tool, and tech names.
evals.expected_output: A slides.txt written beside slides.pdf with one `## slide-NN` section per slide image in order (21 slides), every section non-empty, UTF-8 without a BOM. The skill transcribes the rendered slide PNGs, so it recovers text baked into screenshots — including the `CLAUDE.md` editor view on slide 7 that a PDF text-layer extractor misses. Product/tech names appear verbatim with exact casing: ChatGPT, Cursor, Claude Code, CLAUDE.md, Ruby on Rails, Vue JS, Payboy, HRMS, StackOverflow. The source slides.pdf and the slides/ PNGs are left untouched.
assertions: slides.txt exists beside the source slides.pdf
slides.txt parses into ## slide-NN sections, one per slide (count == staged PNG count)
Section headers are zero-padded, 1-based, contiguous: slide-01..slide-NN
No empty sections in slides.txt
slides.txt is UTF-8 without a BOM
The term 'ChatGPT' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Cursor' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Claude Code' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'CLAUDE.md' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Ruby on Rails' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Vue JS' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Payboy' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'HRMS' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'StackOverflow' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The source slides.pdf is byte-identical to the fixture (sha256 unchanged)
The slide PNGs under slides/ are untouched (sha256 unchanged)

evals.name: khew
evals.prompt: I have a talk's slide deck rendered as images in {WORK_DIR}/slides/ (the source PDF is at {WORK_DIR}/slides.pdf). Please pull the deck's text out into a single reusable plain-text file beside the PDF, so later steps can build a term glossary from it without re-reading the slide images. Keep the exact spelling and casing of product, tool, and tech names.
evals.expected_output: A slides.txt written beside slides.pdf with one `## slide-NN` section per slide image in order (6 slides), every section non-empty, UTF-8 without a BOM. This deck is image-only, so the skill transcribes the slides/ PNGs. Tech names appear verbatim with exact casing: IaC, Infrastructure as Code, Terraform, Bicep, Ansible, Markdown. The source slides.pdf and the slides/ PNGs are left untouched.
